principle: One global charter. Local law-change beachheads. Rights only expand (Art. 114).

jurisdictions.name: Canada
jurisdictions.summary: Federal campaign commitment, federal Commons paper petition, MP briefing pack, official e-petition pending MP sponsor.
jurisdictions.legalNote: Campaign signatures are civic commitments, not House of Commons e-petition signatures until an e-### is live.

jurisdictions.name: United Kingdom
jurisdictions.summary: Next English-language beachhead after Canada traction. Likely path: UK Parliament petitions + model language for Online Safety / privacy alignment — not copy-paste of Canadian paper forms.
jurisdictions.legalNote: UK petition rules, data protection (UK GDPR), and Parliamentary process differ from Canada. Do not collect wet signatures under Canadian forms for UK use.
jurisdictions.nextMilestone: Publish UK-specific legal brief + petition path after Canada proof points

jurisdictions.name: European Union
jurisdictions.summary: EU path is multi-member-state: GDPR alignment narrative is strong, but petitions and legislation run through national and EU institutions. Charter remains model language, not a regulation draft.
jurisdictions.legalNote: No EU-wide paper petition equivalent to Canadian Commons sheets. Member-state campaigns must be designed per country with counsel.
jurisdictions.nextMilestone: Pick 1–2 pilot member states + FR/DE briefing packs

jurisdictions.name: Global charter
jurisdictions.summary: Anyone on Earth can read, sign locally, stamp the hash, and translate. No nationality required for the moral charter.
jurisdictions.legalNote: Moral signature is not a court filing or government petition.
